context: Tests whether the agent implements a Ruby service object using the correct .call class method pattern, standardized hash response format, proper file placement, and frozen_string_literal pragma — the foundational conventions for any service object.

checklist.name: Self.call delegation
checklist.description: The service class defines `def self.call(params)` that delegates to `new(params).call` (or equivalent)

checklist.name: Instance call method
checklist.description: There is a separate instance `def call` method that performs the actual work

checklist.name: Success response key
checklist.description: Success return value contains a `success:` key set to `true`

checklist.name: Success response nesting
checklist.description: Success return value wraps payload inside a `response:` key (e.g. `{ success: true, response: { ... } }`)

checklist.name: Error response key
checklist.description: Failure return value contains a `success:` key set to `false`

checklist.name: Error response nesting
checklist.description: Failure return value wraps error details inside a `response:` key (e.g. `{ success: false, response: { error: { ... } } }`)

checklist.name: File location
checklist.description: Service file is placed under `app/services/<module_name>/` directory, not directly in `app/services/`

checklist.name: Frozen string literal
checklist.description: Service file begins with `# frozen_string_literal: true`

checklist.name: Module namespace
checklist.description: The service class is wrapped in a module namespace that matches the directory name

checklist.name: No raw exceptions returned
checklist.description: The `.call` / `call` method does NOT raise unhandled exceptions to the caller — errors are caught and returned as hashes
